regions.label: Central Fire Altar
regions.summary: The altar can anchor discussion of ritual mediation, sacred order, and the relation between visible fire and interpretive structure.

notes.heading: Working reading
notes.body: Use this region for passages on fire, ritual authority, ordering principles, and the central place of religious knowledge.

notes.heading: Text cluster
notes.body: Add DD, WZ, PY, or NM passages here when the discussion turns to ritual performance, purity, priestly mediation, or sacred transmission.

regions.label: Upper Dark Opening
regions.summary: The dark oval above the center can mark a threshold, rupture, or receiving point for forces moving between levels.

notes.heading: Interpretive focus
notes.body: Use this region for texts that describe openings, passages, unseen causes, or transitions between spiritual and material orders.

regions.label: Upper Light Flame
regions.summary: The pale flame and smoke at the top can carry discussion of ascent, illumination, cosmic order, and refined spiritual movement.

notes.heading: Discussion slot
notes.body: Add passages here that connect light, ascent, beneficent order, or spiritual purification.

regions.label: Lower Dark Flame
regions.summary: The black flame rising from below can represent pressure from destructive, adversarial, or contaminating forces.

notes.heading: Discussion slot
notes.body: Use this region for material on druz, Ahreman, pollution, violence, disorder, or forces that push upward into the ordered field.

regions.label: Left Dark Smoke
regions.summary: The left smoke mass can gather texts on surrounding pressure, invasion, distortion, or resistance to ordered divisions.

notes.heading: Discussion slot
notes.body: Add passages that describe hostile forces pressing against the ordered world, especially when language suggests surrounding or penetration.

regions.label: Right Dark Smoke
regions.summary: The right smoke mass can hold parallel or contrasting examples of darkness, opposition, and cosmic pressure.

notes.heading: Discussion slot
notes.body: Use this area for paired readings that compare the right and left smoke as mirrored forces or repeated textual motifs.

regions.label: Red Branch Network
regions.summary: The red branches can support discussion of textual branching, spread, relation, growth, or distributed concepts.

notes.heading: Discussion slot
notes.body: Add groups of terms, semantic motifs, or repeated phrases that branch across the corpus.

regions.label: Divided Wheel
regions.summary: The radial divisions can structure categories, stages, oppositions, or sections of the argument.

notes.heading: Discussion slot
notes.body: Use this region for an outline of interpretive categories, conceptual classes, or the sequence of a presentation.
